Files

tai/env/
packages.rs

1// Phase 3: Package manager detection via PATH scan
2
3const KNOWN_MANAGERS: &[&str] = &[
4    "apt", "dnf", "yum", "pacman", "zypper", "apk", "emerge", "brew", "port", "nix", "snap",
5    "flatpak",
6];
7
8/// Given a list of package manager names that were found, return them.
9/// This is the testable core: the caller decides how to check existence.
10pub fn filter_found(candidates: &[&str], checker: impl Fn(&str) -> bool) -> Vec<String> {
11    candidates
12        .iter()
13        .filter(|name| checker(name))
14        .map(|name| name.to_string())
15        .collect()
16}
17
18/// Detect package managers by checking if they exist in PATH.
19pub fn detect() -> Vec<String> {
20    filter_found(KNOWN_MANAGERS, |name| which::which(name).is_ok())
21}
22
23#[cfg(test)]
24mod tests {
25    use super::*;
26
27    #[test]
28    fn filter_found_all_present() {
29        let result = filter_found(&["apt", "brew", "nix"], |_| true);
30        assert_eq!(result, vec!["apt", "brew", "nix"]);
31    }
32
33    #[test]
34    fn filter_found_none_present() {
35        let result = filter_found(&["apt", "brew", "nix"], |_| false);
36        assert!(result.is_empty());
37    }
38
39    #[test]
40    fn filter_found_some_present() {
41        let result = filter_found(&["apt", "brew", "pacman"], |name| name == "brew");
42        assert_eq!(result, vec!["brew"]);
43    }
44
45    #[test]
46    fn detect_returns_vec() {
47        // On any system, this should return a Vec (possibly empty)
48        let managers = detect();
49        // All returned names should be from the known list
50        for mgr in &managers {
51            assert!(KNOWN_MANAGERS.contains(&mgr.as_str()));
52        }
53    }
54
55    #[test]
56    fn known_managers_is_not_empty() {
57        assert!(!KNOWN_MANAGERS.is_empty());
58    }
59}
